title: Dashboard Loading Slowly — Performance Troubleshooting
content:
Slow dashboard loading in CloudDash typically stems from query complexity, time range selection, browser resources, or upstream cloud provider API latency. This guide walks through diagnosis and optimization.

First, identify whether slowness affects all dashboards or a specific one. If all dashboards load slowly, check CloudDash system status at status.clouddash.io for regional incidents. Also test from an incognito browser window to rule out extension conflicts or stale cache. CloudDash caches dashboard queries for 30 seconds by default; hard refresh with Ctrl+Shift+R clears client-side cache.

For specific slow dashboards, open the Query Inspector (click any widget > Inspect > Query Performance). The inspector shows query execution time, data points returned, and the backing cloud provider API. Queries exceeding 5 seconds are flagged. Common causes include: time ranges over 30 days on high-cardinality metrics, more than 20 widgets on a single dashboard, or raw log queries without filters.

Optimization strategies: reduce the time range to 24 hours or less for initial load, replace raw log widgets with pre-aggregated metric widgets, enable query result caching on individual widgets (widget settings > Advanced > Cache Duration), and split large dashboards into focused views with linked navigation.

Network latency to cloud provider APIs affects load times significantly. CloudDash query engines run in the region you selected at signup (US-East, EU-West, or AP-Southeast). If your AWS resources are in ap-south-1 but your CloudDash region is US-East, cross-region API calls add 200-400ms per widget. Consider requesting a region migration via support for Enterprise accounts.

Pro and Enterprise plans include dashboard load performance reports under Analytics > Dashboard Performance, showing P50 and P95 load times over 30 days. Enterprise customers can enable dedicated query nodes for guaranteed sub-2-second dashboard loads.
